headline: Micron Technology (MU) Wins HBM4 Supplier Role For Nvidia Vera Rubin AI Platform
summary: Micron Technology (NasdaqGS:MU) has been approved as an HBM4 supplier for Nvidia's next-generation Vera Rubin AI platform. The approval ties Micron directly to Nvidia accelerators planned for high-volume deployment in the second half of 2026. The agreement focuses on high bandwidth memory for advanced AI workloads where capacity and throughput are critical. Micron Technology sits at the heart of the memory and storage industry, and HBM4 is one of the most demanding products in that...

headline: Oil and US gas prices retreat on US-Iran ceasefire
summary: While US stocks (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) are mixed as investors continue to digest the Trump administration's reported peace deal with Iran, oil prices (CL=F, BZ=F) have sunk below $80 per barrel but remain elevated above pre-war prices. Yahoo Finance Senior Reporters Brooke DiPalma and Ines Ferré assess the impact these geopolitical headlines are having on oil and domestic US gas prices (RB=F).

headline: The 'Dirty Dozen' is the new 'Mag 7': Will OpenAI or Anthropic take the last spot?
summary: The "Dirty Dozen" refers to all the stocks in the "Magnificent Seven," as well as SpaceX (SPCX), Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM), Broadcom (AVGO), and Micron (MU). The final spot will go to either OpenAI (OPAI.PVT) or Anthropic (ANTH.PVT). Barron's associate editor Al Root, Yahoo Finance Breaking Business News Reporter Jake Conley, and Morning Brief host Julie Hyman discuss more in the video above.

headline: Peace Deal Lights the Fuse - Dow Hits Record, Chips Go Vertical
summary: A signed framework, a falling oil price, and a chip index that just broke through a level it had never seen before, Monday gave investors a session that did not require much interpretation. When the geopolitical risk premium evaporates and the rate path stops getting worse, equities tend to do exactly what they did yesterday.
